properties.Name: Dagachhu HEP
properties.Descriptio: It all started with a model of Head race tunnel, transition from channel to pressurized tunnel. Numerical analysis showed that vortex will form at upstream of transition. To verify this, the transparent Perspex physical model was built, to observe the flow pattern inside the transition. The air entraining vortex was observed at high flows. To eliminate this, an anti-vortex device in form of a beam was suggested. The location and sizing of beam was optimized in the model and implemented successfully on the site as well.

properties.Name: Super Trishuli HPP
properties.Descriptio: A 100 MW Hydro Power Project is planned on the Trishuli River in central Nepal with a Dam Toe Powerhouse, and the project is planning to install very high radial gates. A two-dimensional physical model was built on a scale of 1:55 to evaluate the discharging capacity and performance of the energy dissipation arrangement provided in the form of a stilling basin downstream of the spillway. The model studies highlighted the need to lower the height of the radial gates and add one or two spans of spillways to improve the energy dissipation arrangement. Additionally, improvements in the stilling basin were also suggested.

properties.Name: Nam-E-Moun HPP
properties.Descriptio: Nam-E-Moun Hydro power project having an installed capacity of 128 MW in Laos PDR, Two Rivers were reproduced for length of around 1 km, on a Freudian model scale ratio of 1:40. Both the rivers have a peculiarity that they are having very steep banks with high vegetation cover consisting of tall trees. High  friction  in  order  of  n=0.1  was reproduced for estimation of the water levels during high floods Model trees and pebbles, grit, were used to reproduce the high friction along the banks. Its effect on the river course was evaluated. The  water  levels  in  both  the  rivers  were calibrated. After  due  calibration  of  the  river  models, spillway and intake structures were installed in the river.

properties.Name: Allain Duhangan HPP, Manali
properties.Descriptio: The 192 MW run-of-the-river Hydro Power Plant, located in Manali, operates with a net head of 860 m. The current arrangement of heat exchangers downstream of the turbines experiences heavy sediment deposition, which adversely affects its performance and efficiency. To address this, a physical hydraulic Froudian model was constructed at a 1:10 scale, and sediments were simulated within the model. The observations revealed that low velocities in the vicinity of the heat exchangers were responsible for the heavy sedimentation around the units. Based on these findings, it was understood that increasing both turbulence and velocities would facilitate effective silt removal. Consequently, a structural intervention consisting of an arrangement of tees and beams in the upstream and downstream vicinity of the heat exchangers was determined to be the most suitable solution for this purpose.

properties.Name: Phata Byung HEP
properties.Descriptio:
Phata Byung Hydro Electric Project is a run-of-river hydroelectric project proposed on the Mandakini River near Sitapur village in Rudraprayag district, Uttarakhand. The revised project envisages the construction of an 18 m high concrete gravity dam. Two low-level sluice gates, each measuring 8.5 m × 11 m with a crest level at EL 1619.0 m, have been provided to safely pass flood discharge under all-gates-operating conditions.
The hydraulic model studies were conducted on a 1:35 scale geometrically similar Froude model to evaluate the performance of the spillway, diversion tunnel, and silt flushing tunnel under various operating conditions.
The studies focused on assessing the upstream and downstream flow conditions near the dam, the discharge capacities of the spillway and diversion/silt flushing tunnels, and the pressure distribution over these hydraulic structures. The overall hydraulic behaviour and operational performance of the spillway system under different flow scenarios were also evaluated.
properties.Photos: images/Phata/Phata Byung HEP.jpg|images/Phata/Phata Byung HEP1.JPG|images/Phata/Phata Byung HEP2.jpg

properties.Name: Nam-E-MounHPP
properties.Descriptio: Hydraulic Model Study

properties.Name: Chorokhi River mouth, Batumi
properties.Descriptio: Perhaps one of the biggest models ever constructed in the laboratory, it spread over 70 m x 30 m, and a river length of 2.5 km was reproduced along with some part of the seabed. A part of the coast near the mouth of the Chorokhi river has been eroding for the last few decades. The geometrically similar rigid bed physical hydraulic model on the scale of 1:50, conforming to Froudian similitude, provided good insight in assessing the hydraulic performance and sediment movement near the Chorokhi river mouth. A mathematical model in HEC-RAS was prepared for the reach of 3 km from the river mouth. The mathematical model was used to arrive at and compare the water surface profiles and Manning’s n values in various sections of the river reach, and the results of the mathematical and physical models were in close agreement. The diversion of the river mouth was found feasible by bodily shifting the cross sections from 400 m upstream of the river mouth towards the north side. Appropriately designed guide bunds along with proper protection works designed for maximum velocities are required on the left bank of the main stream as well as the connecting stream. After diversion, the sediment deposition pattern observed near the mouth changed with respect to its location and extent, and it was observed that fine sediments having a size of 0.1 mm to 1 mm were deposited near the right side of the flow near the mouth.

properties.Name: Pakal Dul HPP
properties.Descriptio: The Pakal Dul HEP envisages construction of a 167 m Concrete Faced Rockfill Dam with an installed power capacity of 1000 MW on the Marusudar River, a tributary of the Chenab River, in the Kishtwar District of Jammu and Kashmir, India. Hydraulic model studies were conducted on a geometrically similar 1:40 scale model for the Tunnel Spillway, Power Intake Bulkhead, and Service Gates. The model studies were initially conducted to assess the overall hydraulic performance of the water conductor system for both the Tunnel Spillway and Power Intake. Further, detailed experiments were carried out to estimate hydrodynamic forces and flow conditions in the gate wells, including the upstream and downstream transitions.

properties.Name: Nagalwadi LIS Pump Sump
properties.Descriptio: Lift irrigation scheme on the Narmada River at Nagalwadi. The entire scheme envisages seven stage pumping. The main pump intake consists of 5 V.T. Pumps. A physical model on scale of 1:12 was built. Studies were conducted for pump sump, to eliminate the flow circulation and vortex formation. A computational fluid dynamics (CFD) model was also carried out for this pumping station. The physical and mathematical CFD model showed very good correlation.

properties.Name: Haliyal Pump Sump
properties.Descriptio: To evaluate the suitability of the proposed design and layout of the pump intake structure. To ensure swirl free flow condition at suction bell mouth of stage and observe levels in the pump sump along with approach velocities. Model studies were conducted on a 1:6 scale model for different pump operation conditions at two water levels; MDDL and MDDL +3 m. Studies were conducted to evaluate and modify the approach flow conditions and eliminate the flow circulation and vortices in front of pump bays.

properties.Name: Nam-Ngao HPP
properties.Descriptio: Hydraulic Model Study

properties.Name: Annaram(Saraswathi) Barrage
properties.Descriptio:
The studies were conducted on 1:45 scale 2D sectional model for assessing the efficacy of the existing stilling basin and alternative proposals for improving the dissipation in the form of baffle blocks, raised end sill and extended stilling basin. Hydraulic performance of the extended stilling basin was acceptable in terms of protecting the existing structure and diverting the high velocity flows of the order of 8 to 15 m/s downstream of the existing stilling basin by length of 50 m, thereby reducing the velocities in the range of 3 to 5 m/s.
The experiments were conducted for the original design of the barrage, assessing discharging capacity for free flow over the barrage and for assessing the discharging capacity for various gate openings and reservoir levels and modifications to the existing end sill with EL 104.5.

properties.Name: Baggi HEP
properties.Descriptio: Hydraulic Model Study

properties.Name: Nam Beng, HPP
properties.Descriptio: Hydraulic Model Study

properties.Name: Nam Ngiep2C HPP
properties.Descriptio: Hydraulic Model Study

properties.Name: Hydraulic Design of Diversion Canal-XekongRiver
properties.Descriptio: Hydraulic Model Study
properties.Photos: images\Xekong\Hydraulic Design of Diversion Canal-XekongRiver.jpeg|images\Xekong\Hydraulic Design of Diversion Canal-XekongRiver1.png|images\Xekong\Hydraulic Design of Diversion Canal-XekongRiver2.png|images\Xekong\Hydraulic Design of Diversion Canal-XekongRiver3.jpeg

properties.Name: Nam Houng1 HPP
properties.Descriptio: Hydraulic Model Study

properties.Name: Vijaynagar PSP
properties.Descriptio:
The Vijaynagar PSP (130 MW) is located in Bellary district of Karnataka. It is proposed on two reservoirs i.e., Upper reservoir which is to be constructed and the existing lower reservoir.
Two separate physical hydraulic models with scales of 1:25 and 1:35 for comprehensive models of Upper intake structure and downstream water conductor system including surge chamber and lower intake respectively are constructed to conduct comprehensive physical hydraulic model studies.
The studies were conducted on 1:35 scale 3D comprehensive model of lower intake structure and downstream water conductor system. The model were observed for steady state conditions and transient conditions.
The model studies aim to assess the hydraulic performance of intake structures, including their shape, submergence depth, and potential for vortex formation. Also the evaluation of the need and effectiveness of anti-vortex devices under various operating modes. Additional objectives include analyzing velocity distribution, pressure profiles during transients, and surge behavior in the system. The influence of entrance geometry and approach flow conditions on intake performance was also examined.

properties.Name: Pinnapuram PSP
properties.Descriptio:
Pinpuram Pumped Storage Project (PSP) is located in Kurnool District, Andhra Pradesh. Hydraulic model studies are being carried out at our laboratory for the Upper Intake Structure of the 1680 MW project.
The upper intake structure comprises a concrete-lined approach channel of approximately 973 m in length and 190 m in width, seven horizontal diffuser-type intake units with gate shafts, and seven inclined circular steel-lined penstock tunnels, each having a diameter of 7.5 m.
The model studies are being undertaken to evaluate the hydraulic performance of the upper intake structure by assessing flow conditions, velocity distribution, flow uniformity, pressure characteristics, and intake performance under various operating conditions. The studies will also examine the effectiveness of the intake geometry and approach flow conditions to ensure efficient and safe operation of the project.

properties.Name: Ganjem Usgao
properties.Descriptio: Hydraulic Model Study

properties.Name: Ratale HEP
properties.Descriptio: Hydraulic Model Study

properties.Name: Lapchi Khola
properties.Descriptio: Hydraulic Model Study

properties.Name: Kameng HEP
properties.Descriptio: Hydraulic Model Study

properties.Name: Saundatti Lower Reservoir
properties.Descriptio:
A 1:40 scale geometrically similar Froude model was developed for the Saundatti PSP Lower Intake structure to assess hydraulic performance, flow conditions, pressure profiles, and velocities, covering the lower reservoir, tail pool, intake structure, gate shafts, and tail race tunnel up to the powerhouse.
Pressure losses remained within acceptable limits (0.0–0.3 m in generation mode and 0.6–0.9 m in pumping mode), while intake velocities ranged 0.50–0.90 m/s.
No significant vortex formation or hydraulic short-circuiting was observed; however, minor flow non-uniformity during generation mode indicates scope for intake modifications.

properties.Name: Saundatti Upper Reservoir
properties.Descriptio:
A 1:40 scale 3-D physical hydraulic model based on Froude similitude was developed for the Saundatti PSP Upper Intake structure, covering the upper reservoir, inlet pool, intake structure, gate shafts, and 75 m long penstocks. 
Flow conditions were acceptable in both modes, with no significant vortices observed and intake velocities ranging from 0.55 – 0.79 m/s.
The upper intake structure exhibited satisfactory hydraulic performance with smooth flow conditions and acceptable pressure losses (1.5–1.6 m in generation mode; 0.6–0.7 m in pumping mode).
The overall intake design was hydraulically sound; however, improving transition near the gate shaft is recommended for better pumping flow conditions.

properties.Name: ShongTong Karcham HEP
properties.Descriptio:
The physical hydraulic model for the Silt Flushing Tunnel (SFT), including Branch Flushing Conduits (BFCs), was constructed as a geometrically similar model at a 1:25 scale for a prototype length of 1117.40 m to study the revised geometry of the SFT.
The model studies were carried out for different sediment concentrations ranging from 3000 to 5500 ppm, using coconut shell powder as the sediment material, having a specific gravity of 1.2.
Overall flow conditions along the SFT were uniform, and no return flow was observed. Higher flow velocities of approximately 10–13 m/s were recorded. All sediment injected at the inlet of the BFCs was completely flushed away due to these high velocities.
The current revised design of the Silt Flushing Tunnel (SFT), including its transitions and alignment, is hydraulically efficient for sediment flushing.

properties.Name: MP30 Gandhisagar PSP
properties.Descriptio:
The physical hydraulic model of the Lower Intake Structure, including the tailrace channel and a portion of the lower reservoir, is being constructed as a geometrically similar model at a 1:50 scale.
The model comprises a part of the lower reservoir, the entire tailrace channel over a prototype length of 2350 m, including the tail pool, the lower intake structure with the gate shaft, and the tailrace tunnels up to a prototype length of 50 m.
The hydraulic model studies are being carried out under both Generation Mode and Pumping Mode of turbine operation.
The studies are intended to evaluate the hydraulic performance of the lower intake structure by assessing flow patterns, velocity distribution, flow conditions, and other relevant hydraulic parameters under both operating modes.
Initially, the model studies were conducted on the original design provided by the project authorities. Subsequently, divide walls were introduced to improve flow separation, and the studies were repeated to assess the hydraulic performance of the modified lower intake structure.

properties.Name: Boras barrage HEP
properties.Descriptio:
A 1:60 Scale Physical Hydraulic Model Studies for Boras Barrage (25 MW) including barrage, powerhouse, intake, and tailrace system.
Hydraulic Optimization of Intake System through guide wall and channel modifications for stable and uniform flow conditions.
Enhanced Powerhouse Performance ensuring efficient operation at design discharge conditions.
Tailrace & Flow Behavior Assessment with recommendations for hydraulic improvements.
Sediment Management Strategy developed through flushing and operational studies for sustainable performance.
